subject: Social Science (History)
chapter_name: The Making of the National Movement: 1870s–1947
overall_learning_objectives: Understand the meaning of nationalism and national movement
Identify the early political organizations formed in India
Understand the formation and role of the Indian National Congress
Recognize the contributions of important national leaders
Explain the role of mass movements in the freedom struggle
Understand how people from different regions joined the movement
Recognize the importance of independence and democratic values

lesson_plan.title: Introduction to the National Movement
teaching_strategy: Class discussion on freedom and independence
student_activities: List reasons why people wanted independence

lesson_plan.title: Early Political Associations
student_activities: Prepare a short timeline of early political events

lesson_plan.title: Formation of the Indian National Congress
topics_covered: Establishment of the Indian National Congress
student_activities: Write a short note on the Indian National Congress
Discuss the goals of the early Congress leaders

lesson_plan.title: Growth of the National Movement
teaching_strategy: Use of pictures of national leaders
student_activities: Identify famous leaders of the freedom movement
Share what they know about one national leader

lesson_plan.title: Mass Movements for Freedom
student_activities: Discuss how people joined the freedom movement
Write a short paragraph about unity in the movement

lesson_plan.title: Towards Independence
student_activities: Prepare a simple timeline from 1870s to 1947

lesson_plan.title: Impact of the National Movement and Revision

teacher_tip: Use stories about freedom fighters and important events of the independence movement to make the lesson engaging. Encourage students to connect the struggle for independence with the values of unity, democracy, and responsibility in present-day India.
